home.heroTitle: Teaching, research, and software in one place
home.heroLead: The site is moving from a companion demo to a clearly structured workspace: courses, research topics, and software artifacts each get their own entry point.
home.focusText: The first iteration adopts structure and material from the lectures wiki and puts exercises, slides, videos, and project-related resources directly onto the course pages.
home.navigationText: Instead of a generic study hub, the home page now leads directly into the three main areas.
home.nextText: Content is gradually moving into language files and importable source data. That makes it easier to add more courses without expanding code-bound translations.

teaching.label: Teaching
teaching.title: Courses with direct access to exercises and materials
teaching.intro: The current iteration adopts structure and content from the lectures wiki. Course pages should not just list links, but keep the most relevant working materials within immediate reach.

research.label: Research
research.title: Research themes that connect teaching and software
research.intro: This area will grow into an overview of research themes, project ideas, and entry points for theses. In the first step it establishes the future structure next to the already expanded teaching section.

software.label: Software
software.title: Teaching software, open-source building blocks, and reusable packages
software.intro: This area collects the tools, apps, and libraries that emerge from teaching and research. The current companion work is the technical starting point for that layer.

areas.title: Teaching
areas.summary: Courses with dedicated subpages, exercises, slides, videos, and supporting materials.

areas.title: Research
areas.summary: Focus areas, research themes, and entry points for projects and theses.

areas.title: Software
areas.summary: Teaching software, open-source artifacts, and reusable packages from projects and courses.

researchCards.title: AI, software engineering, and didactic tooling
researchCards.text: Research topics will later be connected with projects, artifacts, and clear contact points.

researchCards.title: Projects and theses
researchCards.text: The plan is to expose visible topic windows for student work and collaborative development projects.

researchCards.title: From research prototype to teaching software
researchCards.text: The page should later show how demonstrators, benchmarks, and learning software emerge from research work.

softwareCards.title: Interactive learning tools
softwareCards.text: Companion applications and their importable content should become visible here in one place.

softwareCards.title: NuGet and PyPI artifacts
softwareCards.text: Reusable libraries and helper tools will later receive a curated overview here.

softwareCards.title: Project landscape
softwareCards.text: Selected repositories will later be described with context, purpose, and usage instead of being listed as bare links.

lectures.title: App Development
